mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in real GDP growth reduces equity risk premiums by confirming economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising GDP periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings expectations, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 9.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates and rising real estate construction activity indicate accommodative financial conditions fueling asset price inflation, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 when FEDFUNDS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or forward returns are flat across the top and bottom terciles of the interaction signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production reflects recovering supply capacity that outpaces demand, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic strength, reducing discount rates and equity risk premiums to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising payroll periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in housing starts signals strong consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where housing starts jump by more than 50 units.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of rising consumer credit balances and falling unemployment indicates a credit-fueled consumption boom that overheats the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as policy tightening looms.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains positive or flat when RSAFS and UNRATE both rise, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high and low interaction signal terciles.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in consumer credit balances reflects deleveraging and reduced purchasing power, compressing corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where consumer credit falls below the threshold.
